questions.text: Choose the sentence with the correct use of the comparative degree of an adjective:
questions.subject: English

options.text: My bed is more big than my desk.
options.explanation: Incorrect: "More big" is incorrect; "bigger" is the correct comparative form.

options.text: My bed is bigger then my desk.
options.explanation: Incorrect: "Then" is incorrect; the correct word is "than" when making comparisons.

options.text: My bed is bigger than my desk.
options.explanation: Correct: "Bigger than" is the correct comparative structure.

options.text: My bed is more bigger than my desk.
options.explanation: Incorrect: "More bigger" is incorrect because "bigger" is already a comparative form.

questions.explanation: Correct: "Bigger than" is the correct comparative structure.

questions.subject: English

options.text: I now feel sadly and inadequately that I don’t have enough money to buy the books I want.
options.explanation: "Sadly" and "inadequately" Are adverbs, but "sad" And "inadequate" should be used as adjectives here.

options.text: I now feel sad and inadequate that I don’t have enough money to buy the books I want.
options.explanation: The "sad" And "inadequate" are adjectives and grammatically correct and follows proper word order. The infinitive “to buy the books I want” clearly shows the purpose of needing money.

options.text: I now feel sad and inadequate that I don’t have money enough to buy the books I want.
options.explanation: "Money enough" is grammatically incorrect; it should be "enough money."The adjective should me placed before noun.

options.text: I now feel sad and inadequate that I have not enough money buying the books I want.
options.explanation: “Have not enough money” is incorrect word order; it should be “do not have enough money.”The phrase “money buying the books” is wrong because it lacks “to buy,” making the meaning unclear.

questions.explanation: The "sad" And "inadequate" are adjectives and grammatically correct and follows proper word order. The infinitive “to buy the books I want” clearly shows the purpose of needing money.
